_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Severely symptomatic patients with Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy (HOCM) despite optimal medical therapy
Presence of dynamic left ventricular outflow obstruction (LVOTO)

preauth.label: Echo/ color Doppler report with stills

claim.label: Post procedure echo/colour Doppler report and angio stills

ppd_preauth.q: Was the ECG and echo report by cardiologist suggestive of Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy?

cpd_claim.q: Did the post procedure echo/ colour doppler report confirm decrease in septal thickness?

common_queries: Echo/colour Doppler report not suggestive of Ventricular outflow obstruction.
ECG and Echo report by cardiologist not suggestive of Hypertrophic Obstructive Cardiomyopathy.
Post-procedure Echo/colour Doppler report does not confirm a decrease in septal thickness.
Clinical notes lack detailed history, signs, symptoms, or clear indication for Percutaneous Transluminal Septal Myocardial Ablation.

clinical_key_pointers: Hypertrophic obstructive cardiomyopathy (HOCM) is a clinical condition where there is asymmetrical hypertrophy of antero-basal septum resulting into dynamic left ventricular outflow obstruction.

The obstruction is secondary to systolic anterior motion of the mitral valve abutting against a hypertrophied septum, resulting in diastolic dysfunction, myocardial oxygen supply-demand mismatch, reduced forward output, and secondary mitral regurgitation.
